taxonomy: Tretorhiza tibetica is a genus of flowering plants in the family Apocynaceae. This genus is native to the Himalayas and Tibet. Tretorhiza tibetica is further classified into the tribe Orobancheae. The exact classification within the Apocynaceae family may be subject to ongoing research and updates. Further studies are needed to confirm its classification at the species level.
uses: Tretorhiza tibetica, a plant native to the Tibetan Plateau, has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments. It is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with fever and infections. The plant's roots are often used in traditional Tibetan medicine to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Additionally, Tretorhiza tibetica has been studied for its possible antitumor and antimicrobial activities. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
